text.title: Browser Automation Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped browser-automation work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every click followed by sleep(500); suite passes locally and fails on slow CI runners 30% of the time.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used sleep to wait for animations.
antipattern.fix: Replace each sleep with an explicit assertion on the next expected state. See rule no-sleep-only-waits.

antipattern.symptom: Designer updates Tailwind classes; 40 tests break the next morning.
antipattern.fix: Add data-test-id on every interactive element; key selectors on test ids. See rule selector-priority.

antipattern.symptom: Sign-up form rename forces edits across 12 test files.
antipattern.fix: Introduce SignUpPage class; concentrate selectors there. See rule page-object-isolation.

antipattern.symptom: Suite flake rate creeps from 1% to 8%; team treats CI red as random noise.
antipattern.fix: Track flake_rate; fail merge when > 2% over a 100-run window. See rule flake-budget-enforced.
